content.value: For every node, all values in its left subtree are smaller and all values in its right subtree are larger. This property makes search, insert, and delete run in O(h) time, where h is the tree's height - O(log n) for a balanced tree, but O(n) for a degenerate (skewed) one.

items: Leaf node (no children): simply remove it
One child: replace the node with its single child
Two children: replace the node's value with its inorder successor (smallest value in the right subtree), then delete that successor node

content.value: You can use either the inorder successor (min of right subtree) or the inorder predecessor (max of left subtree) for the two-children case - both are correct, just be consistent and state which one you're using.

quiz.question: In a BST, where are values smaller than a node stored?
When deleting a node with two children, what do you do?
options: Replace it with its inorder successor (or predecessor), then remove that node

quiz.question: What is BST search's time complexity in the worst case (skewed tree)?
